Abstract.AbstractText: Creatine is a popular and widely used ergogenic dietary supplement among athletes, for which studies have consistently shown increased lean muscle mass and exercise capacity when used with short-duration, high-intensity exercise. In addition to strength gains, research has shown that creatine supplementation may provide additional benefits including enhanced postexercise recovery, injury prevention, rehabilitation, as well as a number of potential neurologic benefits that may be relevant to sports. Studies show that short- and long-term supplementation is safe and well tolerated in healthy individuals and in a number of patient populations.
Abstract.CopyrightInformation: Copyright © 2021 by the American College of Sports Medicine.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Sports Medicine, UConn Primary Care Sports Medicine Fellowship, Department of Orthopedics, UConn Health, Farmington, CT.
Department of Family Medicine, University of Connecticut, St. Francis Hospital, Hartford, CT.
Department of Family Medicine, University of Connecticut, St. Francis Hospital, Hartford, CT.
